basic_details.job_title: FRI Dehradun Recruitment 2026 - Apply Walkin for 11 Project Assistant, Field Assistant and More Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Retired Technical Officer/Retired Range Officer (RTO/RRO), Senior Research Fellow (SRF), Senior Project Fellow (SPF), Junior Project Fellow (JPF), Project Assistant (PA), Field Assistant (FA)
basic_details.short_information: Forest Research Institute (FRI), Dehradun, under the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE), has released a walk-in interview notification (Advertisement No. 5-1/RCS/2026 (Advert.)/(July), dated 17.07.2026) for temporary fellowship and project positions. The walk-in interviews will be held on 28 July 2026 and 29 July 2026 at the Board Room, FRI Main Building, P.O. New Forest, Dehradun - 248006. This article explains the FRI Dehradun recruitment 2026 details, eligibility, fellowship amount, documents required, and how to attend the walk-in interview, based strictly on the off

important_dates.raw: Notification Date: 17 July 2026 Interview Day 1: 28 July 2026 (RTO/RRO, SPF, JPF, SRF posts) Interview Day 2: 29 July 2026 (PA and FA posts) Registration Window: 9:00 AM to 10:15 AM on each respective interview date | No separate call letter or interview intimation letter will be issued to candidates. Eligible and interested candidates must report directly on the notified dates and times.

age_limit.details: The notification does not specify a separate age limit for Project Assistant and Field Assistant posts.

qualification.details: post: Essential Qualification | rto/rro: Retired Technical Officer or Range Officer who has worked in a forest research organization or forest department under state/central government or autonomous bodies, with at least 25 years' experience in Silviculture and forest management activities | senior project fellow (spf): First Class M.Sc. in Forestry/Botany | junior project fellow (jpf): First Class M.Sc. in Forestry/Agroforestry/Botany | senior research fellow (srf): First Class M.Sc. in Botany/Forestry/Environment Management, plus 2 years' research experience | project assistant (pa) - sal regeneration project: B.Sc. in Forestry/Botany | field assistant (fa): Intermediate (10+2) | project assistant (pa) - singori-bhatwari project: B.Sc. in Biology/Botany (Hons.)/Forestry/Agriculture | project assistant (pa) - medicinal plants project: B.Sc. in Biology/Forestry/Agriculture

salary_details.rto/rro: Rs. 35,000/- consolidated, or Basic Pay as per LPC minus current Basic Pension, whichever is less
salary_details.senior project fellow (spf): Rs. 28,000/- per month for two years
salary_details.project assistant (sal project): Rs. 19,000/- per month (year 1-2); Rs. 20,000/- per month (year 3-5)

selection_process: Selection will be made through a walk-in interview. There is no written exam mentioned in the notification. Candidates need to register between 9:00 AM and 10:15 AM on their respective interview date and submit their documents to the office of the Group Coordinator (Research)
how_to_apply: Check the post-wise eligibility criteria and select the relevant post and date (28 or 29 July 2026).
Prepare bio-data, a recent passport-size photograph, and self-attested copies of all educational and experience certificates (plus LPC and Pension Slip for RTO/RRO applicants).
Reach the Board Room, FRI Main Building, P.O. New Forest, Dehradun - 248006, between 9:00 AM and 10:15 AM on the applicable interview date.
Complete registration and submit documents to the office of the Group Coordinator (Research).
Attend the walk-in interview on the same day.
There is no online application process for this recruitment; candidates must appear in person with the required documents.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for FRI Walk-in Interview 2026
content_sections.text: Notification Date: 17 July 2026 Interview Day 1: 28 July 2026 (RTO/RRO, SPF, JPF, SRF posts) Interview Day 2: 29 July 2026 (PA and FA posts) Registration Window: 9:00 AM to 10:15 AM on each respective interview date No separate call letter or interview intimation letter will be issued to candidates. Eligible and interested candidates must report directly on the notified dates and times.

content_sections.text: The notification does not specify a separate age limit for Project Assistant and Field Assistant posts.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for FRI Walk-in Interview
content_sections.text: Candidates must carry the following documents at the time of registration: Copy of updated bio-data Recent passport size photograph Self-attested copies of all educational certificates Self-attested copies of all relevant/experience documents For the RTO/ RRO post only: Last Pay Certificate (LPC) and recent Pension Slip, in addition to the above documents Candidates who are already employed must bring a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their current/last institute.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be made through a walk-in interview. There is no written exam mentioned in the notification. Candidates need to register between 9:00 AM and 10:15 AM on their respective interview date and submit their documents to the office of the Group Coordinator (Research), P.O. New Forest, FRI, Dehradun, for verification and interview.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply / Attend the FRI Walk-in Interview 2026
content_sections.text: Check the post-wise eligibility criteria and select the relevant post and date (28 or 29 July 2026). Prepare bio-data, a recent passport-size photograph, and self-attested copies of all educational and experience certificates (plus LPC and Pension Slip for RTO/RRO applicants). Reach the Board Room, FRI Main Building, P.O. New Forest, Dehradun - 248006, between 9:00 AM and 10:15 AM on the applicable interview date. Complete registration and submit documents to the office of the Group Coordinator (Research). Attend the walk-in interview on the same day. There is no online application process for this recruitment; candidates must appear in person with the required documents.

faq.question: Q1. What is the FRI Dehradun walk-in interview 2026 about?
faq.answer: It is a walk-in interview conducted by the Forest Research Institute, Dehradun, to fill temporary fellowship and project positions, including RTO/RRO, SRF, SPF, JPF, Project Assistant, and Field Assistant posts, on 28 and 29 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What are the FRI walk-in interview dates for 2026?
faq.answer: The interviews are scheduled on 28 July 2026 and 29 July 2026 at 9:00 AM at the Board Room, FRI Main Building, Dehradun.

faq.question: Q3. What documents should I carry for the FRI walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring a bio-data copy, a recent passport-size photograph, and self-attested copies of all educational certificates and relevant documents. RTO/RRO candidates must additionally carry their LPC and recent Pension Slip.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for this FRI recruitment?
faq.answer: The notification does not mention any application fee, as this is a walk-in interview and not an online application process.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for FRI Dehradun fellowship posts?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 65 years for RTO/RRO, 32 years for SRF/SPF, and 28 years for JPF, as on 01.06.2026. Age relaxation of up to 5 years is available for SC/ST, Women, Physically Handicapped, and OBC candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary/fellowship amount offered?
faq.answer: The fellowship amount ranges from Rs. 19,000 to Rs. 42,000 per month (plus HRA for SRF), depending on the post, as detailed in the fellowship table above.

faq.question: Q7. Is a separate call letter issued for the interview?
faq.answer: No. The notification states that no separate letter will be issued for attending the interview; candidates must report directly as per the schedule.

faq.question: Q8. Does this fellowship guarantee a permanent job at FRI or ICFRE?
faq.answer: No. The notification specifies that the award of the fellowship does not guarantee subsequent employment in any institute under the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE).
